generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE expressions.
Ensure AGREE type declarations (int, real, bool) are correct and resolve references.
Focus on fixing type mismatches reported by the validator by adjusting helper declarations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Range checks are commonly expressed as `eq range_name : bool = x >= lower and x <= upper;`
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new AADL architecture to resolve AGREE validation errors.
Avoid placing guarantee statements within implementation annexes.
Do not attempt to repair syntax errors by modifying unrelated parts of the model.
